Fresh IMC simplifygeometry worker. Use the strongest Pro Extended reasoning available and think deeply for as long as needed; do not answer early. Repository with all history and attachments: https://github.com/lequoctran181/IMC_Challenge_2026 . Problem: Kattis IMC 2026 Problem B simplifygeometry. Input/output modified OBJ triangular closed manifold. Constraints: output closed watertight 2-manifold, nondegenerate faces, valid indices; symmetric Hausdorff is over vertices only within 5% AABB diagonal; visual score is six fixed axial camera SSIM on flat normal/depth maps, threshold 0.9; objective minimize output vertex count. Current best is only about 81.938904; target is 91.80+ / top 1. Many previous QEM/fallback/primitive/duplicate candidates scored 0-60 or plateau 81.93457, so hard-pivot and implement a genuinely new C++17 ready submission. You MUST return an ACTUAL downloadable file named submission_next.cpp with real content, plus a full standalone C++17 code block fallback. Code must compile with g++ -O2 -std=c++17, read stdin/write stdout only, and official sample first line must be exactly 8 12. No prose-only response; produce ready-to-submit code.
Worker slot: Safari tab 6. Seek a distinct breakthrough; do not copy old plateau code.

Show more


I’ll build a real submission_next.cpp and include the full source in the reply. I’ll first inspect the repository context so the candidate avoids the known duplicate families, then compile-check the generated file locally.

Received app response
I found the repository and confirmed it contains prior Kattis/submission source families. I’m going to avoid cloning those patterns and make a structurally different “safe slicer” approach: detect near-box-like/simple cases, otherwise preserve topology with aggressive vertex clustering plus manifold repair and fallback.
